note_data.title: Factorization
keyPoints: Factor — A factor of an algebraic expression is an expression which divides it exactly
Factorization — Factorization is the process of expressing an algebraic expression as a product of its factors
Common factor method — Take out the highest common factor (HCF) from all terms
Grouping method — Group terms with common factors and factorize each group separately
Using algebraic identities — Apply standard identities like a² - b², a² ± 2ab + b², a³ ± b³
Difference of two squares: a² - b² = (a + b)(a - b)
Perfect square trinomials: a² + 2ab + b² = (a + b)² and a² - 2ab + b² = (a - b)²
Sum and difference of cubes: a³ + b³ = (a + b)(a² - ab + b²) and a³ - b³ = (a - b)(a² + ab + b²)
Quadratic trinomials of the form ax² + bx + c can be factorized by splitting the middle term
Always check factorization by expanding the factors back to original expression

definitions.definition: A factor of an algebraic expression is an expression which divides it exactly.
Factorization is the process of expressing an algebraic expression as a product of its factors.
A prime factor is a factor that cannot be factorized further.
A common factor is a factor which is common to all terms of an algebraic expression.
The highest common factor of two or more algebraic expressions is the expression of highest degree which divides each of them exactly.

importantQuestions.question: Define factorization and give an example.
importantQuestions.answer: Factorization is the process of expressing an algebraic expression as a product of its factors. Example: 6x² + 9x = 3x(2x + 3)

importantQuestions.question: Factorize the following expressions: (i) x² - 16 (ii) 4x² + 12xy + 9y² (iii) x³ - 27
importantQuestions.answer:
(i) x² - 16 = x² - 4² = (x + 4)(x - 4) using a² - b² = (a + b)(a - b)
(ii) 4x² + 12xy + 9y² = (2x)² + 2(2x)(3y) + (3y)² = (2x + 3y)² using a² + 2ab + b² = (a + b)²
(iii) x³ - 27 = x³ - 3³ = (x - 3)(x² + 3x + 9) using a³ - b³ = (a - b)(a² + ab + b²)

mcqs.question: Which identity is used to factorize a² + 6a + 9?

commonMistakes: Students forget to check if there's a common factor before applying identities
Confusing a² + 2ab + b² = (a + b)² with a² - 2ab + b² = (a - b)²
Not verifying the factorization by expanding back to original expression
Making sign errors when applying difference of squares formula
Forgetting to factorize completely when multiple methods are needed
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1-2 long questions in every BSEK paper
Always write the identity being used before applying it
Practice recognizing which method to use by looking at the form of expression
In exam, show all steps clearly for partial marks even if final answer is wrong
note_data.keyDiagram: No specific diagram required for this chapter, but students should practice organizing factorization steps in a systematic tabular format showing: Original Expression → Method Used → Identity Applied → Final Factored Form
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to factorize 4x² - 9y² using difference of squares method
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (5 marks) — required factorization of quadratic expression ax² + bx + c by grouping method
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (3 marks) — tested factorization by taking out common factors from algebraic expressions
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q6 (4 marks) — asked to factorize perfect square trinomials and verify the answer

workedExample.question: Factorize: 6x² + 11x + 3 (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify coefficients a = 6, b = 11, c = 3
Step 2: Find factors of ac = 18 that add to b = 11 (9 and 2)
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct method, 1 mark for finding factor pairs, 1 mark for grouping correctly, 1 mark for final answer

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by not showing complete steps in factorization - always write each step clearly as per STBB textbook method
Common mistake is incorrect signs when factoring - double-check by expanding the factored form to verify your answer
Many students confuse factorization methods - clearly identify whether to use common factors, grouping, or special products before starting
note_data.quickRecap: Factorization is expressing an algebraic expression as a product of its factors. Main methods include: taking out common factors, grouping method, and using special products (difference of squares, perfect square trinomials). Always verify your answer by expanding the factored form. Choose the appropriate method based on the structure of the given expression.
importantFormulas: a² - b² = (a + b)(a - b) [Difference of squares]
x² + (a + b)x + ab = (x + a)(x + b) [Factoring quadratics]
